what_it_does: Defines the Design DNA Pack project charter and operating manual: its purpose, non-goals, architecture layers, runtime model, folder state, methodology, roadmap, open decisions, and quality gates.
observations: Frames the project as a compositional assembly system and language for generating identity-consistent interfaces, content, and copy.
States that all visual and verbal dimensions are tokens, including size, space, colour, proportion, line, texture, motion, and voice.
Defines a layered architecture: primitives, registries, templates, scales and dials, voice, skills and context, invariants, and provenance.
Separates source content from reusable language, repeatedly saying product story and domain copy are scaffolding to discard.
Describes an AI runtime loop that resolves controls, loads a relevant slice, composes, generates, verifies, and logs provenance.
Documents current and target folder structures, including evidence, tokens, registries, templates, scales, voice, skills, probes, and dist.
Records later project layers for surface UI, layouts, sequence, registry circuit, voice, and spaces.
Contains status, roadmap, known gaps, open decisions, and quality gates in the same file as the charter.
standouts: Treats identity as a queryable, loadable system rather than a static style guide.
Makes provenance and evidence discipline central to token creation.
Extends the DNA model across visual composition, verbal voice, page sequence, reader context, and AI loading rules.
Uses invariants as the bridge between parametric variation and recognisable output.
conventions: Uses numbered sections with later lettered extensions such as 8b through 8e.
Uses declarative principles, diagrams, tables, pseudo-records, and folder trees to describe the system.
Separates non-goals, methodology, runtime, roadmap, and quality gates into explicit sections.
Uses token-style names and JSON-like identifiers throughout even though the file itself is prose.
Emphasizes content-free extraction, evidence labels, and provenance for sampled values.
concerns: The file combines charter, specification, roadmap, implementation notes, API notes, and historical status, making its maintenance scope broad.
The section hierarchy becomes uneven after section 8, with multiple appended subsections carrying substantial architecture.
Several current-state and status statements are time-sensitive and may need regular updates to remain accurate.
notes: Briefing someone on this file: it is the project-level operating map for turning observed design language into a tokenized, generative DNA Pack with AI loading rules.
questions: From this file alone, what exact output format should the consuming engine use for dist/?
From this file alone, which typeface is confirmed as source-accurate?
From this file alone, how should inferred tokens graduate to confirmed tokens?
From this file alone, what exact review process decides when a probed template becomes part of the pack?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file says it is the project charter and operating manual, and it actually defines the project's purpose, architecture, operating loop, structure, status, roadmap, and quality gates.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to preserve an authored design language as a machine-consumable system that can generate new artifacts without copying source content.
novelty: Distinctive in its treatment of brand/design identity as a multi-layer generative circuit spanning tokens, registries, templates, sequence, voice, reader context, provenance, and AI skills.
contribution: Contributes a neutral project-level schema for what the fused system would keep: the pack purpose, layer model, runtime flow, evidence discipline, target structure, operating loop, quality gates, and named open questions.
summary_for_embedding: Project charter and operating manual for a Design DNA Pack that tokenizes visual and verbal identity into primitives, registries, templates, scales, voice, skills, provenance, and invariants for AI-assisted generation.
intention_for_embedding: Written to guide humans and agents in extracting, structuring, testing, and compiling a reusable design language from evidence into a machine-loadable generative pack.
